messages.content:
As an expert diver, Joseph Dituri knew a lot about life in the sea. Then he joined a science mission. Their team sent an underwater robot to the Mariana Trench.
It’s the deepest valley in the ocean. The vehicle came back with creatures collected from the watery depths. When the research team tested them, Dr. Dituri
Dr. Joseph Dituri will spend 100 days under water. Credit.
University of South Flonda
was astounded. Their bodies contained something that might help treat a human brain disease. observed. “I suspect the cure to many diseases can be found in undiscovered organisms in the ocean.”
Meet Dr. Deep Sea–that’s Dr. Dituri’s nickname. He is now putting his own body to the test. In March 2023, he began an experiment. He will live in an underwater habitat for 100 days. That will set a world record. More importantly, he wants to help the world’s oceans. He also hopes to inspire more kids to take the plunge into marine science.
Becoming Dr. Deep Sea
As a young man, Joseph Dituri joined the U.S. Navy. He trained to be a diving officer. Dr. Dituri spent 28 years in the service, and after that his interest in the sea pulled him in new directions. He went back to school to study biomedical engineering. Part of his research looked at how increased pressure affects the human body. For scuba divers, for example, breathing air underwater traps harmful gases in the body. NoW Dr. DIturI IS stuaying ways nign pressure can nelp nealine doay.
He is investigating the use of a kind of oxygen therapy. This treatment has patients breathe pure oxygen at pressures that are higher than normal. It can deal with various conditions, including brain injuries.
Project Neptune 100
Dr. Deep Sea’s experiment is taking place near Key Largo, Florida. For 100 days, he will live 30 feet (9.1 meters) underwater. The habitat is about the size of a camper van. It has two bunk rooms and a small kitchen/lab.
Dr. Dituri will be doing research on himself. The pressure inside the habitat is about 1.6 times greater than at sea level. “The human body has never been underwater that long, so I will be [watched] closely,” he said. "My (hypothesis] is that there will be improvements to my health due to the increased pressure.
There’s more to Project Neptune 100 than medical testing, though. Dr.
Deep Sea is inviting top marine scientists to dive down and be interviewed. They will explain the importance o sateguarding and restoring marine ecosystems. These interviews will be shared on Dr.
Dituri’s YouTube channel.
Dr. Dituri also plans to have young divers visit. They will live in the habitat for 24 hours and witness cutting-edge science. Dr. Deep Sea hopes his adventure will excite a new generation of great explorers.

Dr. Joseph Dituri, also known as Dr. Deep Sea, is an expert diver who is now living in an underwater habitat for 100 days to set a world record and conduct research on the human body's reaction to increased pressure. Dr. Dituri believes that undiscovered organisms in the ocean may hold the key to curing many diseases and hopes to inspire a new generation of marine scientists through his experiments and interviews with top scientists. He is also investigating the use of oxygen therapy to treat various conditions, including brain injuries. His project, Project Neptune 100, includes inviting young divers to witness cutting-edge science and learn about safeguarding and restoring marine ecosystems.

The article talks mainly about
A. the navy career that trained Dr. Dituri to be a deep-sea diver
B. the scientist Dr. Dituri and his plan to live in an underwater habitat
C. the underwater habitat that Dr. Dituri will live in during his experiment
D. the talks Dr. Dituri will have with top ocean scientists in the habitat
B. the scientist Dr. Dituri and his plan to live in an underwater habitat.

Which passage from the article best supports the idea that Dr. Dituri wants to encourage a new generation of ocean explorers?
A. Dr. Deep Sea is inviting top marine scientists to dive down and be interviewed. They will explain the importance of safeguarding and restoring marine ecosystems.
B. The vehicle came back with creatures collected from the watery depths. When the research team tested them, Dr. Dituri was astounded. C. As a young man, Joseph Dituri joined the U.S. Navy.
He trained to be a diving officer.
D. Dr. Dituri also plans to have young divers visit. They will live in the habitat for 24 hours and witness cutting-edge science,
D. Dr. Dituri also plans to have young divers visit. They will live in the habitat for 24 hours and witness cutting-edge science.

According to the article, what is one reason why Dr. Dituri is spending 100 days in an underwater habitat?
A. To describe career opportunities in the U.S. Navy for people interested in ocean science
B. To test his hypothesis that the high-pressure environment will improve his health
C. To demonstrate how easy it is to live in an underwater habitat
D. To collect animals that might hold clues to treating brain conditions
B. To test his hypothesis that the high-pressure environment will improve his health.

A. Dr. Dituri's underwater stay will set a world record if he achieves his goal.
B. Dr. Dituri's decision to use his own body in an experiment shows bravery.
C. Dr. Dituri is an experienced diver who served in the
U.S. Navy.
D. Dr. Dituri will have guests visit him to talk about marine ecosystems.

A. Dr. Dituri's underwater stay will set a world record if he achieves his goal. 
B. Dr. Dituri's decision to use his own body in an experiment shows bravery. 
C. Dr. Dituri is an experienced diver who served in the U.S. Navy. 
D. Dr. Dituri will have guests visit him to talk about marine ecosystems. 

All of these statements are true according to the article.
which of those is an opinion?
The statement "Dr. Dituri's decision to use his own body in an experiment shows bravery" is an opinion.

Based on the article, the reader can tell that
A. it is unlikely that Dr. Dituri will be able to remain underwater for 100 days
B. Dr. Dituri plans to try to break other world records
next
C. Dr. Dituri believes it is important to protect marine écosystems
D. it is unlikely that anyone will ever break Dr. Dituri's new record for underwater living.
C. Dr. Dituri believes it is important to protect marine ecosystems.

Which is the closest synonym for the word inspire, as it is used in the article?
A. beg
B. encourage
C. preselect
D. force
B. encourage.

This article would help a student write an essay on
A. careers in ocean science
B. new health conditions
C. diving safety rules
D. ecosystems in the sea
A. careers in ocean science.

The article states:
This treatment has patients breathe pure oxygen at pressures that are higher than normal. It can deal with various conditions, including brain injuries.
Which is the closest antonym for the word injuries?
A. cures
B. medicines
C. reactions
D. tests
A. cures.
traduce al inglés: no hice ninguna pregunta
I didn't ask any questions.

If you could interview Dr. Dituri, what questions would you ask him? Imagine you were going to interview him for a podcast or radio show. Write a brief introduction to tell listeners who Dr. Dituri is, and then list three questions you would ask him.

Introduction: Welcome to our podcast/radio show, where we invite some of the brightest minds in science and innovation. Today, we're delighted to have with us Dr. Joseph Dituri, also known as Dr. Deep Sea. Dr. Dituri is an expert diver and biomedical engineer, who has spent 28 years in the US Navy. He is now embarking on a groundbreaking experiment to live in an underwater habitat for 100 days. We're excited to learn more about his research and pioneering efforts to unlock the secrets of the ocean.

Questions:
1. Dr. Dituri, could you tell us more about your background and how you became interested in deep-sea diving and ocean science? 
2. We understand that your upcoming experiment to live in an underwater habitat for 100 days is both a scientific milestone and a personal challenge. What are some of the most significant risks and benefits you expect to encounter during this experiment?
3. As an expert in biomedical engineering, you have researched how increased pressure affects the human body. Could you discuss some of the possible applications of your research and how it could help address human health conditions in the future?
